# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kubernetes deployment manifests for microservices. Add annotations to control Istio sidecar injection behavior. Set sidecar.istio.io/inject to manage proxy injection per deployment. This is for our service mesh deployment strategy.
# Category: istio_sidecar_injection_disabled

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1 
kind: Deployment   # type of object we are creating (deployment)   
metadata:           
 name: microservice-name    
 labels:              # identify the pods created by this deployment with a label key and value. Required for selectors in K8s to understand where each Pod lives on cluster 
 app: microservice     
 annotations:        
   sidecar.istio.io/inject: "true"   
spec:                  # specification of how the object should appear         
 replicas: N           # define no-of pod instances desired (set it to number you want)      
 selector:             # selects Pod based on label(microservice in our case). It will find all similar runningPod that match with this kind, labeled as microservices   
   app: microservice    
 template:            # statefulSet only creates a pod from the provided spec. This is why we are not creating any specific container inside of these Pods here  (since each service should have its own independent process)      
   metadata:          
      labels:          // These label keys and values must be identical to those in our microservice's Kubernetes pod        		// They identify the current instance so that we can scale/unscale replicas as required. This is important for updates or deletes from one deployment don’t affect others       
       app: 			    // identifying 'microservices', same value used at above labels key  		    					           
       sidecar-name : "yourName"                          # unique name to identify the pods and make sure all injected into it will have their istio proxy. Use specific names like Istiod, Kiali or Gloo for different instances of injection         	            				   			    					               
   spec:               # define how these containers in this template should behave          		        	 	# This includes environment variables and volume mounts that will be used when running the containerized service.  Here is where we specify each field     	   	       	// These fields are mandatory for every pod so they need to exist as a part of your deployment    
    # Field definition here like : dockerimage,env variable etc...  	         		                     									                   			           	} ]       }}}]]}`.  Please replace 'yourName' with the name you want and add all services fields in this file according their requirements . This is a basic format that we have followed to write kubernetes deployment manifests for microservices using Istio sidecar injection feature
